---
name: Lexicon / name / brand contribution
about: Propose a new entry for the transliteration lexicon, named-entity list, or brand dictionary
title: "[LEXICON] "
labels: lexicon
assignees: ''
---

## Word / phrase

The romanized surface form exactly as it appears in Hinglish text (include common alternate spellings if any):

```
<e.g. "Zomato", "WhatsApp", "chai", "gully">
```

## Script of origin

- [ ] English loanword / brand name
- [ ] Hindi / Urdu (romanized)
- [ ] Other: ___

## Proposed Devanagari form

**display** (for reading):

```
<e.g. ज़ोमाटो>
```

**tts** (phoneme / pronunciation hint, if different from display):

```
<e.g. zo-maa-to>
```

## Category

Which lexicon category does this belong to?

- [ ] `BRAND` — product / company name
- [ ] `PERSON` — personal name
- [ ] `PLACE` — city, region, landmark
- [ ] `COMMON_NOUN` — everyday Hindi/Urdu word
- [ ] `SLANG` — colloquial / informal term
- [ ] `OTHER`: ___

## Justification / source

Why should this entry be added? Link to a dictionary, news article, official brand page, or frequency data:

- Source 1:
- Source 2 (optional):

## Existing behavior (before this entry)

What does `normalize("<word>")` currently return for this surface form?

```
display: 
tts: 
category: 
```

## Additional notes

Alternate spellings, regional variants, or any special casing rules to be aware of.
